tests.output_sample:
Local-store aggregation that tells you whether a 'near' or 'goat' query
on this city is running on thin data. For each registered v1 source, prints
the row count from the last sync, the last-synced timestamp, the trust
weight, and (for regional sources) whether the country-match boost applies.
Missing sources surface as zero rows so an agent can decide to call
sync-city before querying.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli coverage <city-slug> [flags]

Examples:
  # Quick coverage check for Tokyo
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli coverage tokyo

  # Pipe to jq for agent consumption
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli coverage paris --json --select coverage.source,coverage.row_count

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for coverage

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'wanderlust-goat-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

Spatial cross-entity SQL: pairs places where the first member matches one
intent (default: food) and the second matches another (default: culture or
historic), within --pair-distance meters of each other AND within the
--radius meters of the anchor. Ranked by combined trust × proximity.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli crossover [flags]

Examples:
  # Default food+culture pairing in Marais
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli crossover --anchor "Marais, Paris" --radius 800m

  # Food + viewpoint pairing
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli crossover --anchor "Asakusa, Tokyo" --radius 1km --pair food+viewpoint

  # Agent JSON for pipelines
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli crossover --anchor "Bukchon, Seoul" --radius 1.2km --pair food+culture --agent

Flags:
      --anchor string     Anchor address or "lat,lng".
  -h, --help              help for crossover
      --min-trust float   Minimum trust for both members (default 0.85). (default 0.85)
      --pair string       Intent pair: food+culture, food+historic, food+viewpoint. (default "food+culture")
      --radius string     Search radius (e.g. 800m, 1km, 1.2km). (default "800m")

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'wanderlust-goat-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

tests.output_sample:
Heuristic GOAT compound. Identical fanout to 'near' but explicitly
guarantees no LLM is invoked at runtime: criteria phrases are translated to
OSM tag filters and Reddit body keywords through static lookup tables in
internal/criteria/. Use 'goat' from shell pipelines, cron jobs, and any
context where you cannot or should not delegate to an agent.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli goat <anchor> [flags]

Examples:
  # Lat,lng anchor with no agent in the loop
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli goat 35.6895,139.6917 \
    --criteria "vintage clothing, vinyl, hidden" --minutes 20

  # CSV mode for cron-driven shortlists
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli goat "Marais, Paris" \
    --criteria "natural wine, no scene" --minutes 15 --csv

Flags:
      --criteria string   Free-text criteria (e.g. "hand-pulled noodles, locals only").
  -h, --help              help for goat
      --identity string   Free-text identity — informs ranking but does not invoke an LLM.
      --minutes int       Walking-time radius in minutes. (default 15)

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'wanderlust-goat-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

geocode returned no results for "__printing_press_invalid__"

Pure-Go SunCalc-style sun-position math (no external API) plus a
walking-radius viewpoint search. Returns:
- sunrise / sunset / civil dawn / civil dusk
- evening blue hour and golden hour windows (and morning equivalents)
- viewpoints from the local store ranked by elevation tag and Reddit
  accessibility hits.

Use --zone to display in a specific local time zone (e.g. Asia/Tokyo).

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli golden-hour <anchor> [flags]

Examples:
  # Tonight's blue-hour windows + Tokyo viewpoints
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli golden-hour "Tokyo Tower" --date 2026-06-15 --zone Asia/Tokyo

  # Lat,lng + minutes-radius
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli golden-hour 48.8584,2.2945 --date 2026-06-21 --minutes 30 --agent

Flags:
      --date string   Date YYYY-MM-DD (default: today). (default "2026-05-07")
  -h, --help          help for golden-hour
      --minutes int   Walking-time radius in minutes for viewpoint search. (default 20)
      --zone string   IANA zone for displayed times (e.g. Asia/Tokyo, Europe/Paris). (default "Local")

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output

      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

Import data from a JSONL file by issuing POST requests for each record.
Each line must be a valid JSON object. Failed records are logged to stderr
but do not stop the import.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli import <resource> [flags]

Examples:
  # Import from a JSONL file
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli import <resource> --input data.jsonl

  # Dry-run to preview without sending
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli import <resource> --input data.jsonl --dry-run

  # Import from stdin
  cat data.jsonl | wanderlust-goat-pp-cli import <resource> --input -

Flags:
      --batch-size int   Records per batch (future: batch API support) (default 1)
      --dry-run          Preview import without sending requests
  -h, --help             help for import
  -i, --input string     Input JSONL file path (use - for stdin)

tests.output_sample:
Error: required flag(s) "input" not set
required flag(s) "input" not set

Persona-shaped local fanout. Geocodes the anchor (address or "lat,lng"),
then fans out to every source eligible for the location's country. Scores by
trust × (1 + country_match_boost) × intent_match × walking-time-decay against
the local SQLite store + live source results, returning 3-5 ranked picks
with local-language names preserved alongside transliterations.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli near <anchor> [flags]

Examples:
  # Persona-shaped 15-minute walk near a Tokyo hotel
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli near "Park Hyatt Tokyo" \
    --criteria "vintage jazz kissaten, no tourists, great pour-over" \
    --identity "coffee snob, into 70s Japanese kissaten culture" \
    --minutes 15

  # By lat,lng with --agent for JSON output
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli near 35.6895,139.6917 \
    --criteria "viewpoint, blue hour" --identity "photographer" \
    --minutes 20 --agent --select results.name,results.walking_min

Flags:
      --criteria string   Free-text criteria (e.g. "vintage jazz kissaten, no tourists").
  -h, --help              help for near
      --identity string   Free-text identity (e.g. "coffee snob, into 70s kissaten culture") — boosts local-language sources.
      --minutes int       Walking-time radius in minutes (default 15, max 60). (default 15)

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)

Reverse geocode lat/lng to a structured address.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli places reverse [flags]

Examples:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli places reverse --lat 42 --lng 42

Flags:
      --addressdetails int   Return structured address parts (1=on, 0=off). Default: 1. (default 1)
      --format string        Response format. Always 'json' in this CLI. (default "json")

Cross-source content pattern: matches goat_reddit_threads body + title for
quiet-signal phrases (dead before, empty weekday, quiet on, never crowded,
almost empty, rarely busy, no line) joined to goat_places, optionally
filtered by walking radius. The result is "places where locals say it's
empty at this time" — one of the most-cited unstated tastes in r/Tokyo
and r/Paris "real cafe" threads.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli quiet-hour <anchor> [flags]

Examples:
  # Quiet kissaten near Yurakucho on a Monday at 2pm
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli quiet-hour "Yurakucho, Tokyo" --minutes 15 --day mon --time 14:00

  # JSON for an agent
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli quiet-hour "Marais, Paris" --minutes 20 --day tue --time 15:30 --agent

Cross-table join: searches goat_reddit_threads body and title for any string
that appears as a name or name_local on a place in goat_places. Returns the
verbatim title + body chunks with subreddit, score, and permalink. No LLM
summarization — agents that need 'real talk' from locals get the raw text
with provenance.

Emit a JSON-shaped query plan for an agent to execute. The plan resolves
the anchor through Nominatim, classifies criteria, and lists every typed
client call (with parameters, locale, expected trust) the agent should run.
The CLI does not execute the plan — that's the agent's job. Drop into an
agent loop to fan out across editorial + Wikipedia + Reddit + OSM + the
country's local-language sources without re-deriving the dispatch each call.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli research-plan <anchor> [flags]

Examples:
  # Emit the plan and pipe to jq
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli research-plan "Bukchon Hanok Village, Seoul" \
    --criteria "hand-pulled noodles, locals only" --identity "food traveler" --json

  # Narrow to client + method only
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli research-plan "Marais, Paris" \
    --criteria "natural wine, no scene" \
    --json --select sources,calls.client,calls.method

Flags:
      --criteria string   Free-text criteria for the plan.
  -h, --help              help for research-plan
      --identity string   Free-text identity (informational only — does not change the plan structure).
      --minutes int       Walking-time radius in minutes (drives radius_meters in each call). (default 15)

Global Flags:

OSRM walking polyline (cached) + spatial buffer query against the local
goat_places store. Returns places within --buffer meters of the polyline
geometry, ranked by trust × proximity-to-path. Geometric local-store query
no single API can answer.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli route-view <from> <to> [flags]

Examples:
  # Walk from Shibuya to Yoyogi Park, see what's worth a 150m detour
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli route-view "Shibuya Station, Tokyo" "Yoyogi Park, Tokyo" --buffer 150m

  # Pipe to jq for an agent
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli route-view "Place de la République, Paris" "Marais, Paris" --buffer 200m --agent

Sync data from the API into a local SQLite database. Supports resumable
incremental sync (only fetches new data since last sync) and full resync.
Once synced, use the 'search' command for instant full-text search.

Exit codes & warnings:
  Resources the API denies access to (HTTP 403, or HTTP 400 with an
  access-policy body) are reported as warnings rather than failing the
  run. In --json mode each is emitted as a {"event":"sync_warning",...}
  line carrying status, reason, and message fields, and a final
  {"event":"sync_summary",...} aggregates the run.

  Exit 0 when at least one resource synced and no resource flagged in
  the spec as critical (x-critical: true) failed; non-critical failures
  emit {"event":"sync_warning","reason":"exit_policy_default_changed",
  ...} so callers can detect that a partial failure was tolerated. Pass
  --strict to exit non-zero on any per-resource failure. Exit is always
  non-zero when every selected resource failed, regardless of --strict.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync [flags]

Examples:
  # Sync all resources
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync

  # Sync specific resources only
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync --resources channels,messages

  # Full resync (ignore previous checkpoint)
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync --full

  # Incremental sync: only records from the last 7 days
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync --since 7d

  # Parallel sync with 8 workers
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync --concurrency 8

  # Latest-only: refresh head of each resource, no historical backfill
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync --latest-only

Flags:
      --concurrency int     Number of parallel sync workers (default 4)
      --db string           Database path (default: ~/.local/share/wanderlust-goat-pp-cli/data.db)
      --full                Full resync (ignore previous checkpoint)
  -h, --help                help for sync
      --latest-only         Refresh head of each resource only; clears resume cursor and caps pages at 1. Mutually exclusive with --since (--since wins).
      --max-pages int       Maximum pages to fetch per resource (0 = unlimited; cap-hit emits a sync_warning event) (default 100)
      --resources strings   Comma-separated resource types to sync
      --since string        Incremental sync duration (e.g. 7d, 24h, 1w, 30m)
      --strict              Exit non-zero on any per-resource failure (default: only critical failures or all-resource failure exit non-zero).

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'wanderlust-goat-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

tests.output_sample:
Pull every layer for a city into the local SQLite store. Run this two
weeks before a trip; afterwards you can use --data-source local to query
without internet. Layers covered: editorial (Eater best-of), Atlas Obscura
city listings, OSM Overpass POIs (heritage, michelin, viewpoint, historic),
Wikipedia GeoSearch (locale-aware), Wikivoyage, /r/<city> top threads.

Known city slugs (v1): tokyo, kyoto, osaka, seoul, busan, paris, lyon,
marseille. Other slugs fall through to a Nominatim geocode of the slug.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync-city <slug> [flags]

Examples:
  # Heavy pre-trip sync of Tokyo
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync-city tokyo --layers all --concurrency 2

  # Refresh only Reddit threads from the last 7 days
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync-city paris --layers reddit --since 7d

  # Custom slug — geocode the term and treat as the city center
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli sync-city "Mexico City"

Flags:
      --concurrency int   Concurrent fetches; keep low for polite rate limiting (default 2). (default 2)
  -h, --help              help for sync-city
      --layers string     Comma-separated layers to sync: all,osm,wikipedia,wikivoyage,reddit,atlasobscura,eater (default "all"). (default "all")
      --since string      Skip rows synced more recently than this (e.g. 7d, 24h, 1w). Empty means full re-sync.

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --compact              Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string   Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string       Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --json                 Output as JSON
      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'wanderlust-goat-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration     Request timeout (default 30s)
      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

which resolves a natural-language capability query (for example, "search messages" or "stale tickets") to the best matching command from this CLI's curated feature index.

Exit codes:
  0  at least one match found
  2  no confident match - the query did not score against any indexed capability; fall back to '--help' or 'search' if this CLI has one

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli which [query] [flags]

Examples:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli which "stale tickets"
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli which "bottleneck"
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli which --limit 1 "send message"
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli which                                # list the full capability index

Flags:
  -h, --help        help for which
      --limit int   Maximum number of matches to return (default 3)

Global Flags:
      --agent                Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)

      --dry-run              Show request without sending
      --human-friendly       Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent           Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op

      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

tests.output_sample:
Step-by-step audit of why a place appears (or doesn't) in a 'near' or 'goat'
result. Resolves the input by name (FTS match in goat_places) or by id, then
prints every source row referencing it, the trust contribution per source,
and the formula breakdown:

  goat_score = trust × (1 + country_match_boost) × intent_match × walking_decay

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli why <place-name-or-id> [flags]

Examples:
  # By exact id
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli why "wikipedia.ja:42519"

  # By name (FTS-style)
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli why "Kohi Bibi" --json

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for why

Archive fetches all syncable resources from the API and stores them in a
local SQLite database. Supports incremental sync (only new data since last run)
and full resync. After archiving, use 'search' for instant full-text search.

Usage:
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli workflow archive [flags]

Examples:
  # Archive all resources
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli workflow archive

  # Full re-archive (ignore previous sync state)
  wanderlust-goat-pp-cli workflow archive --full

Flags:
      --db string   Database path (default: ~/.local/share/wanderlust-goat-pp-cli/data.db)
      --full        Full re-archive (ignore previous sync state)
